chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-342 of 2022 Const. Petition No.D-453 of 2022 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main case 08-08-2023 Mr. Ghulam Mujtaba Jakhar, Advocate for the Petitioners Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant Advocate General.-.-.-. -.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Petitioners are low grade employees working with respondent No.3/Khairpur Medical College District Khairpur under the health department in grade 1 to 4 against different posts. Their case is that they have been rendering services in the College in the said grades on daily wages/contract basis for the last ten years. When the time came for fulfillment of the said posts on regular basis they were ignored. It has been admitted by respondents during the course of hearing that against the subject posts no strict education qualification or expertise of a particular nature are required except formal fitness and suitability. And such criteria in view of long experience of the petitioners with college is largely fulfilled and covered. It is further stated by the officials that there are almost 55 to 57 posts lying vacant in grade 1 to 4 including sweepers and sanitary workers. Against the said posts, the petitioners working, are 32. It has been proposed and agreed by the parties that in terms of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and relevant rules an advertisement would be made in the newspapers inviting applications from the people at large for appointment against those posts. However, the petitioners working in the said grades will be given priority and their services confirmed in consideration of their long experience.

It has been proposed and agreed by the parties that in terms of standard operating procedures (SOPs) and relevant rules an advertisement would be made in the newspapers inviting applications from the people at large for appointment against those posts. However, the petitioners working in the said grades will be given priority and their services confirmed in consideration of their long experience. This statement, made from the side of the respondents, is made part of the court’s order for enforcement. The counsel for the petitioners is satisfied over such arrangement and seeks disposal of these petitions in the above terms. The petitions therefore, are accordingly disposed of in the terms as stated above. Office is directed to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned connected matter. JUDGE JUDGE Sulemen Khan/PA
